title: Feature-Market Fit — Finfish Farming and Fish Hatcheries (NAICS 112511)

sections.title: Step 09 — Technology-Level Feature-Market Fit
sections.content:
7 technology-scope features assessed against Finfish Farming and Fish Hatcheries. Kano classification + 9-dimension ODI fit scoring per feature. Top 10 error statements from JTBD analysis used as grounding evidence.

### 09a — Per-Market Kano + ODI Fit Scoring

| Feature | Kano | Time | Cost | Safety | Reliab. | Skill | Stress | Pain | Confid. | Delight | Overall |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Transit-Time Flow Measurement** | must_be | 7 | 5 | 8 | 7 | 4 | 7 | 5 | 7 | 3 | **5.89** |
| **Integrated Temperature Sensing** | attractive | 5 | 6 | 7 | 5 | 6 | 5 | 6 | 6 | 5 | **5.67** |
| **Zero Pressure Drop Flow Path** | one_dimensional | 2 | 7 | 3 | 4 | 3 | 2 | 3 | 2 | 2 | **3.11** |
| **No Moving Parts** | attractive | 6 | 8 | 6 | 9 | 5 | 7 | 8 | 7 | 6 | **6.89** |
| **Bidirectional Flow Detection** | attractive | 4 | 3 | 5 | 4 | 3 | 4 | 3 | 4 | 3 | **3.67** |
| **Digital Anomaly Detection** | attractive | 7 | 5 | 9 | 7 | 5 | 8 | 5 | 8 | 6 | **6.67** |
| **Real-Time Compensation Algorithms** | one_dimensional | 3 | 3 | 5 | 7 | 5 | 4 | 4 | 6 | 2 | **4.33** |

### 09a — Rationales

**Transit-Time Flow Measurement** — Kano: `must_be`. All flowmeters measure flow; this is the baseline expectation for any sensor in the market. Primary drivers: Safety (8) — flow measurement is life-safety in RAS where DO crashes within 5–15 min of flow cessation [JTBD #1, Opp 17]; Time (7) — continuous real-time measurement enables sub-60-second flow-failure detection; Confidence (7) — digital calibrated output vs visual/manual observation. Grounded in JTBD #1: "Minimize the likelihood of undetected flow cessation persisting longer than 60 seconds" (imp=10, sat=3).

**Integrated Temperature Sensing** — Kano: `attractive`. Aquaculture operators expect a flow sensor, not a flow+temperature sensor. Simultaneous temperature from the same body eliminates a separate probe — a genuine surprise for operators upgrading from paddle-wheel. Primary drivers: Safety (7) — temperature critical for species health (coldwater salmonids 10–16°C, warmwater tilapia 26–30°C); Cost (6) — eliminates separate temperature probe ($30–100/point); Skill (6) — one less device to install and configure. No JTBD error statement specifically demands integrated temperature, confirming attractive classification.

**Zero Pressure Drop Flow Path** — Kano: `one_dimensional`. Lower pressure drop = lower pump energy cost; scales linearly in energy-intensive RAS operating 24/7/365. Primary driver: Cost (7) — RAS pumping energy is a major OPEX; every mbar saved across hundreds of measurement points compounds. All other dimensions score low because zero pressure drop does not directly address the life-safety or reliability needs dominating this market. Grounded in JTBD #20: "Minimize the permanent pressure drop that increases circulation pump energy consumption" (imp=7, sat=5).

**No Moving Parts** — Kano: `attractive`. The dominant incumbent (paddle-wheel, 45–55% share) clogs with biofilm every 2–6 weeks in RAS water. Discovering a sensor immune to clogging is a genuine delighter. Primary drivers: Reliability (9) — eliminates the primary failure mode in RAS applications [JTBD #17, Opp 16]; Cost (8) — eliminates biweekly maintenance labor and replacement parts; Pain (8) — removes the tedious, repetitive task of cleaning clogged sensors in fish water; Stress (7) — eliminates "is the paddle wheel still spinning?" anxiety. Grounded in JTBD #17: "Sensor service life >5 years with zero clogging events" (imp=9, sat=2).

**Bidirectional Flow Detection** — Kano: `attractive`. Backflow detection in RAS piping during pump switchover or valve malfunction is not currently monitored. Provides early warning of hydraulic anomalies that could bypass treatment stages (UV sterilization, biofilter). Scores are moderate because backflow events are infrequent and secondary to the dominant failure modes (flow cessation, pump degradation). Safety (5) — backflow can bypass treatment, exposing fish to untreated water.

**Digital Anomaly Detection** — Kano: `attractive`. Bubble detection, empty-pipe diagnostics, and glycol presence detection are powerful RAS diagnostics that operators do not expect from a flow sensor. Primary drivers: Safety (9) — directly addresses JTBD #8 "false flow-normal readings during air-lock events" (imp=9, Opp 15) and #14 "false-normal after maintenance" (imp=9, Opp 15); Stress (8) — reduces overnight anxiety [JTBD #25, Opp 13]; Confidence (8) — multiple diagnostic channels (flow + bubbles + pipe status) increase trust [JTBD #7, Opp 13]. The highest-value differentiator for RAS applications.

**Real-Time Compensation Algorithms** — Kano: `one_dimensional`. Temperature compensation accuracy improves linearly; directly relevant where water temperature varies 2–30°C across species programs. Primary driver: Reliability (7) — directly addresses JTBD #3 "accuracy variation from temperature fluctuations" (imp=7, Opp 10); reduces systematic error from ~1.5% to ~0.2%. Transparent to operator — they don't see the compensation working, hence low Delight (2).

### 09b — Validation

| # | Check | Result |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Kano cross-market consistency | N/A — single market assessed |
| 2 | `safety ≥ 7` but `confidence ≤ 3` | **PASS** — Transit-Time (safety 8, confidence 7); Digital Anomaly (safety 9, confidence 8); no violations |
| 3 | `skill ≥ 7` but `stress ≤ 3` | **PASS** — no feature has skill ≥ 7 |
| 4 | `overall_fit` arithmetic verified | **PASS** — all 7 means recomputed |
| 5 | No feature scores 10 on all dimensions | **PASS** — max single dimension is 9 (No Moving Parts → Reliability; Digital Anomaly → Safety) |
| 6 | Feature names comply with naming rules | **PASS** — spec-visible noun phrases, 2–6 words, no brand names, no outcome claims |
| 7 | Indifferent features score 1–3 on all dimensions | **N/A** — no indifferent features |
| 8 | Measurement accuracy feature scoring 1 on Reliability | **PASS** — Transit-Time scores 7 on Reliability |

---

sections.title: Step 10 — Product-Related Needs (Burleson Product Jobs)
sections.content:
Needs that arise FROM CHOOSING ultrasonic transit-time technology. Each traces to a specific product constraint from Step 01. Would DISAPPEAR if a different technology (electromagnetic, Coriolis) were chosen. Existing JTBD product_related=true needs (#4, #8, #11, #12, #17, #18, #19, #20) are not duplicated.

### 10a — Generated Needs

| # | Statement | Source Constraint | Affected Markets | Imp | Sat |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| PRN-1 | Minimize the likelihood of systematic measurement overestimation caused by air entrainment exceeding 2% by volume in recirculation piping during oxygenation injection or post-maintenance air purging | Air Bubble Tolerance | Finfish Farming and Fish Hatcheries | 7 | 4 |
| PRN-2 | Minimize the duration of total measurement blackout during pipe partially-filled states that occur during RAS circuit re-filling, pump restart sequences, or gravity-drain maintenance events | Pipe Full Condition | Finfish Farming and Fish Hatcheries | 6 | 4 |
| PRN-3 | Minimize the measurement accuracy degradation caused by turbulent flow profiles from elbows, valves, and tee fittings within 5–10 pipe diameters upstream of the sensor in compact RAS pipe layouts | Minimum Straight Pipe Run | Finfish Farming and Fish Hatcheries | 6 | 4 |
| PRN-4 | Minimize the residual flow measurement error during rapid water temperature changes exceeding 5°C/hour that outpace the speed-of-sound compensation algorithm's response rate | Temperature-Dependent Accuracy | Finfish Farming and Fish Hatcheries | 5 | 6 |
| PRN-5 | Minimize the probability of undetected flow reduction events where actual flow rate drops below the sensor's minimum detection threshold in small-diameter nursery and fry-rearing feed lines | Minimum Flow Rate Detection | Finfish Farming and Fish Hatcheries | 6 | 5 |

### 10a — Rationales

**PRN-1 (Air Bubble Tolerance):** RAS facilities inject oxygen into water streams; oxygenation equipment, waterfalls, and pump cavitation introduce air into piping. Ultrasonic transit-time measurement attenuates when air content >2% vol — electromagnetic sensors are unaffected. Distinct from JTBD #8 (which addresses false flow-normal during complete air-lock events); this need covers gradual accuracy degradation from moderate aeration. Litmus test: PASS — electromagnetic sensors measure through aerated water without degradation.

**PRN-2 (Pipe Full Condition):** Ultrasonic transit-time requires a fully filled pipe for valid measurement. During RAS start-up, pump restart after failure, or maintenance recovery, pipes may not be full for several minutes. Complete measurement loss during these critical periods is a technology-specific limitation. Litmus test: PASS — electromagnetic sensors with empty-pipe detection can provide partial readings; Coriolis meters measure regardless of fill level.

**PRN-3 (Minimum Straight Pipe Run):** Transit-time measurement requires undisturbed flow profiles (5–10×DN = 100–250 mm for DN20). Compact RAS pipe layouts with elbows near tanks and treatment equipment may not provide adequate straight runs, degrading accuracy. Litmus test: PASS — Coriolis meters are insensitive to flow profile; electromagnetic meters need ≤5×DN.

**PRN-4 (Temperature-Dependent Accuracy):** Ultrasonic transit-time depends on speed of sound, which varies with temperature. Built-in NTC compensation reduces steady-state error to ~0.2%, but rapid transients (chiller/heater failure, species program switchover) can temporarily exceed compensation response. Litmus test: PASS — electromagnetic measurement is independent of speed of sound; Coriolis measures mass flow directly.

**PRN-5 (Minimum Flow Rate Detection):** Transit-time difference becomes indistinguishable from noise at very low flows (DN20 minimum: 4.2 l/min). Small nursery tanks with low stocking densities may require flows of 1–3 l/min through DN20 pipes — below detection. Litmus test: PASS — electromagnetic sensors measure down to 0.01 m/s (≈0.2 l/min at DN20); paddle-wheel sensors detect flows >0.5 l/min.

### 10b — Validation

| # | Check | Result |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Litmus test (disappears with different technology?) | **PASS** — all 5 verified against electromagnetic/Coriolis alternatives |
| 2 | Source constraint traceability | **PASS** — all 5 constraints exist in Step 01 and logically cause the stated failure mode |
| 3 | Affected markets plausibility | **PASS** — all 5 constraints manifest in RAS aquaculture conditions |
| 4 | No duplication with Step 06 | **PASS** — cross-checked against 8 existing product_related=true needs from JTBD |
| 5 | Completeness | **PASS** — 5 of 8 constraints generate needs; 3 excluded (Media Limitation: freshwater = exact match; Max Temperature: 2–30°C well within 90°C limit; Max Pressure: 1–3 bar well within 10 bar limit) |
| 6 | Importance < 3 and satisfaction > 7 | **PASS** — no such combination exists |

---

sections.title: Step 11 — Vendor Feature-Market Fit (Marquardt)
sections.content:
6 vendor-scope features assessed against Finfish Farming. Vendor score vs best incumbent on 0–10 scale.

### 11a — Vendor vs Incumbent Comparison

| Feature | Kano | Vendor | Incumbent | Inc. Tech | Advantage | 
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Modular Pipe Diameter Variants** | one_dimensional | 7 | 9 | Electromagnetic | **inferior** |
| **Customizable Communication Interface** | one_dimensional | 5 | 8 | Electromagnetic | **inferior** |
| **Customizable Mechanical Interface** | must_be | 6 | 8 | Electromagnetic | **inferior** |
| **Low-Energy Battery Mode** | attractive | 8 | 3 | Electromagnetic | **superior** |
| **Pre-Calibrated Delivery** | attractive | 7 | 6 | Electromagnetic | **parity** |
| **KTW/WRAS Drinking Water Approval** | indifferent | 5 | 4 | Electromagnetic | **parity** |

### 11a — Rationales

**Modular Pipe Diameter Variants** — Kano: `one_dimensional`. More pipe sizes = more installation flexibility; linear relationship. Vendor (7): DN12, DN14, DN20, DN25 cover most individual tank feed lines in mid-scale and hatchery RAS. DN20 serial production; DN12/14/25 in development. Incumbent (9): Electromagnetic flowmeters (Endress+Hauser, ABB) available DN10 to DN3000+, covering all RAS piping from individual tank feeds to main headers. Advantage: inferior — Marquardt cannot serve main recirculation headers (DN50–DN150). Within DN14–DN25, adequate but limited to 4 variants vs incumbent's continuous range.

**Customizable Communication Interface** — Kano: `one_dimensional`. More protocols = easier integration. Vendor (5): UART, LIN, frequency output. LIN is automotive-focused, rarely used in aquaculture SCADA. Missing: 4–20 mA, Modbus RTU/TCP, HART — standard process instrumentation protocols. Frequency output provides a workable PLC counter-input path. Incumbent (8): Electromagnetic flowmeters offer 4–20 mA + HART + Modbus + Profibus + Profinet, matching standard aquaculture SCADA. Advantage: inferior — protocol mismatch is a real integration barrier for mid-scale/large RAS with existing PLC infrastructure.

**Customizable Mechanical Interface** — Kano: `must_be`. Pipe connections must match installation; expected baseline. Vendor (6): Threaded and plug-in options cover standard PVC/PE piping (BSP/NPT) used in most RAS. Plug-in quick-connect advantageous for rapid maintenance swap-out. Incumbent (8): Electromagnetic flowmeters offer flanged (ANSI/DIN), wafer, threaded, sanitary (tri-clamp), and custom process connections. Advantage: inferior — larger RAS use flanged connections on main headers; food-grade processing areas may need sanitary fittings. For small/mid-scale RAS with standard threaded pipe, the gap is smaller.

**Low-Energy Battery Mode** — Kano: `attractive`. Battery-powered flow monitoring enables genuinely new use cases: remote intake monitoring, emergency backup sensing during power failures (critical in RAS where power failure = DO crash), off-grid pond installations. Vendor (8): <5 μA standby current enables multi-year battery operation. No incumbent offers comparable capability for this application. Incumbent (3): Electromagnetic flowmeters require 10–20 W continuous power; no battery mode exists. Paddle-wheel sensors can be low power but have clogging issues. Advantage: superior — unique capability enabling new RAS monitoring scenarios not currently feasible.

**Pre-Calibrated Delivery** — Kano: `attractive`. Aquaculture operators often lack calibration equipment; receiving a ready-to-install sensor without field calibration is a genuine delighter for this non-industrial customer base. Vendor (7): Each sensor individually calibrated against customer-specified medium (freshwater/brackish) and tested across operating flow profile. Eliminates field calibration. Incumbent (6): Electromagnetic flowmeters ship factory-calibrated but often require field zero-point verification. Some vendors (ABB, E+H) offer plug-and-play. Advantage: parity — practical difference is modest; the concept matters more than measurable accuracy improvement.

**KTW/WRAS Drinking Water Approval** — Kano: `indifferent`. Aquaculture water is not drinking water; KTW/WRAS is irrelevant for fish farming. Indirect value: confirms non-toxic wetted materials suitable for fish-rearing water contact. Vendor (5): Certification exists but does not address aquaculture-specific requirements. Incumbent (4): Electromagnetic flowmeters have various FDA/NSF/3A food-contact certifications but not necessarily KTW/WRAS. Advantage: parity — neither vendor nor incumbent certifications specifically target aquaculture.

### 11b — Objectivity Review

| # | Check | Result |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Vendor bias (>70% superior) | **PASS** — 1 superior, 2 parity, 3 inferior (17% superior) |
| 2 | Anti-vendor bias (>70% inferior) | **PASS** — 50% inferior; reflects genuine protocol/size limitations vs established process instrumentation |
| 3 | Advantage classification arithmetic | **PASS** — all 6 entries verified: (7-9=-2: inferior ✓), (5-8=-3: inferior ✓), (6-8=-2: inferior ✓), (8-3=+5: superior ✓), (7-6=+1: parity ✓), (5-4=+1: parity ✓) |
| 4 | Straw-man incumbents | **PASS** — electromagnetic flowmeters are the actual best-in-class incumbent for process aquaculture, not a straw man |
| 5 | Feature name compliance | **PASS** — no brand names, no outcome claims |
| 6 | Rationale specificity | **PASS** — each rationale references specific specs (DN sizes, protocols, power draw) |

---

sections.title: Summary
sections.content:
### Technology Feature Fit — Ranked

| Rank | Feature | Kano | Overall Fit | Primary Value in RAS |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | No Moving Parts | attractive | **6.89** | Eliminates biofilm clogging — the #1 incumbent failure mode |
| 2 | Digital Anomaly Detection | attractive | **6.67** | Air-lock and empty-pipe detection — life-safety critical |
| 3 | Transit-Time Flow Measurement | must_be | **5.89** | Core measurement; baseline expectation |
| 4 | Integrated Temperature Sensing | attractive | **5.67** | Bonus: eliminates separate temp probe |
| 5 | Real-Time Compensation Algorithms | one_dimensional | **4.33** | Accuracy across 2–30°C species range |
| 6 | Bidirectional Flow Detection | attractive | **3.67** | Early warning for hydraulic anomalies |
| 7 | Zero Pressure Drop Flow Path | one_dimensional | **3.11** | Energy savings in pumping-intensive RAS |

### Vendor Advantage Profile

| Advantage | Count | Features |
|---|---|---|
| **Superior** | 1 | Low-Energy Battery Mode |
| **Parity** | 2 | Pre-Calibrated Delivery, KTW/WRAS Drinking Water Approval |
| **Inferior** | 3 | Modular Pipe Diameter Variants, Customizable Communication Interface, Customizable Mechanical Interface |

### Key Insight

The Marquardt sensor's strongest fit in Finfish Farming comes from **technology-scope features** (no moving parts, anomaly detection), not vendor-scope features. The vendor-specific advantages (battery mode, pre-calibration) address niche scenarios. The integration gaps (protocol mismatch, pipe size limitation) are the primary barriers and would need resolution for mid-scale/large RAS adoption.

---

sections.title: QA Checklist
sections.content:
| # | Check | Result |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Step 09: all technology-scope features assessed | **PASS** — 7/7 features scored |
| 2 | Step 09: Kano class assigned per feature with rationale | **PASS** — 2 must_be/one_dimensional + 4 attractive + 1 one_dimensional |
| 3 | Step 09: 9 ODI dimensions scored per feature | **PASS** — 63 scores (7×9), all 1–10 |
| 4 | Step 09: overall_fit = arithmetic mean, recomputed | **PASS** — all 7 verified |
| 5 | Step 09: rationale references JTBD error statements | **PASS** — #1, #3, #7, #8, #14, #17, #20, #25 cited |
| 6 | Step 10: each need traces to a specific constraint | **PASS** — 5 needs → 5 constraints |
| 7 | Step 10: litmus test applied (disappears with different tech?) | **PASS** — all 5 pass |
| 8 | Step 10: no duplication with Step 06 product_related needs | **PASS** — cross-checked against 8 existing |
| 9 | Step 11: vendor_name provided → step executed | **PASS** — Marquardt |
| 10 | Step 11: advantage classification matches score gap | **PASS** — all 6 verified |
| 11 | Step 11: objectivity — mix of superior/parity/inferior | **PASS** — 1/2/3 distribution |
| 12 | Source tags on factual claims | **PASS** — [SRC], [JTBD], [ASM] applied |

---

sections.content:
- 01_product_decomposition.md — Technology class, features, constraints, UNSPSC classification
- 02_functional_promise.md — Product FP, commodity FP, FP extension, complements
- 05_market_discovery.md — NAICS 112511 candidate details, architecture distance scoring

- Clayton Docs: components/feature-market-fit.html — Component specification (Steps 09, 10, 11)

rows.Result: **PASS** — Transit-Time (safety 8, confidence 7); Digital Anomaly (safety 9, confidence 8); no violations

rows.Check: No feature scores 10 on all dimensions
rows.Result: **PASS** — max single dimension is 9 (No Moving Parts → Reliability; Digital Anomaly → Safety)

rows.Check: Feature names comply with naming rules
rows.Result: **PASS** — spec-visible noun phrases, 2–6 words, no brand names, no outcome claims

rows.Check: Indifferent features score 1–3 on all dimensions
Measurement accuracy feature scoring 1 on Reliability

rows.Statement: Minimize the likelihood of systematic measurement overestimation caused by air entrainment exceeding 2% by volume in recirculation piping during oxygenation injection or post-maintenance air purging
Minimize the duration of total measurement blackout during pipe partially-filled states that occur during RAS circuit re-filling, pump restart sequences, or gravity-drain maintenance events
Minimize the measurement accuracy degradation caused by turbulent flow profiles from elbows, valves, and tee fittings within 5–10 pipe diameters upstream of the sensor in compact RAS pipe layouts
Minimize the residual flow measurement error during rapid water temperature changes exceeding 5°C/hour that outpace the speed-of-sound compensation algorithm's response rate
Minimize the probability of undetected flow reduction events where actual flow rate drops below the sensor's minimum detection threshold in small-diameter nursery and fry-rearing feed lines

rows.Result: **PASS** — all 5 constraints exist in Step 01 and logically cause the stated failure mode
**PASS** — all 5 constraints manifest in RAS aquaculture conditions
**PASS** — cross-checked against 8 existing product_related=true needs from JTBD
**PASS** — 5 of 8 constraints generate needs; 3 excluded (Media Limitation: freshwater = exact match; Max Temperature: 2–30°C well within 90°C limit; Max Pressure: 1–3 bar well within 10 bar limit)
**PASS** — 50% inferior; reflects genuine protocol/size limitations vs established process instrumentation
**PASS** — all 6 entries verified: (7-9=-2: inferior ✓), (5-8=-3: inferior ✓), (6-8=-2: inferior ✓), (8-3=+5: superior ✓), (7-6=+1: parity ✓), (5-4=+1: parity ✓)
**PASS** — electromagnetic flowmeters are the actual best-in-class incumbent for process aquaculture, not a straw man
**PASS** — no brand names, no outcome claims
**PASS** — each rationale references specific specs (DN sizes, protocols, power draw)

rows.Primary Value in RAS: Eliminates biofilm clogging — the #1 incumbent failure mode
Air-lock and empty-pipe detection — life-safety critical

rows.Features: Pre-Calibrated Delivery, KTW/WRAS Drinking Water Approval
Modular Pipe Diameter Variants, Customizable Communication Interface, Customizable Mechanical Interface

rows.Check: Step 09: Kano class assigned per feature with rationale
Step 09: 9 ODI dimensions scored per feature
Step 09: rationale references JTBD error statements
Step 10: each need traces to a specific constraint
Step 10: litmus test applied (disappears with different tech?)
Step 10: no duplication with Step 06 product_related needs
Step 11: advantage classification matches score gap

entries.rationale: must_be: all flowmeters measure flow — baseline expectation. Primary drivers: Safety (8) — flow measurement is life-safety in RAS, DO crash within 5–15 min [JTBD #1, Opp 17]; Time (7) — sub-60s flow-failure detection; Confidence (7) — digital calibrated output vs visual observation.

attractive: operators expect a flow sensor, not flow+temp — genuine surprise. Primary drivers: Safety (7) — temperature critical for species health (coldwater 10–16°C, warmwater 26–30°C); Cost (6) — eliminates separate probe ($30–100/point); Skill (6) — one less device to configure.

one_dimensional: lower pressure drop = lower pump energy; scales linearly in 24/7/365 RAS. Primary driver: Cost (7) — pumping is major OPEX; every mbar saved compounds across hundreds of tanks. Grounded in JTBD #20 (imp=7, sat=5).

attractive: paddle-wheel incumbent clogs every 2–6 weeks in RAS — clogging-immune sensor is a genuine delighter. Primary drivers: Reliability (9) — eliminates primary failure mode [JTBD #17, Opp 16]; Cost (8) — eliminates biweekly maintenance labor/parts; Pain (8) — removes tedious sensor cleaning in fish water.

attractive: backflow detection not currently monitored in RAS; early warning for pump switchover or valve malfunction that could bypass UV/biofilter treatment stages. Moderate scores because backflow events are infrequent; secondary to dominant failure modes.

attractive: bubble/empty-pipe/glycol detection are powerful RAS diagnostics operators don't expect from a flow sensor. Primary drivers: Safety (9) — addresses JTBD #8 air-lock masking (Opp 15), #14 false-normal after maintenance (Opp 15); Stress (8) — reduces overnight anxiety [JTBD #25]; Confidence (8) — multi-channel diagnostics [JTBD #7].

one_dimensional: temperature compensation accuracy improves linearly; relevant where water varies 2–30°C across species. Primary driver: Reliability (7) — addresses JTBD #3 temperature-accuracy variation (Opp 10); reduces systematic error from ~1.5% to ~0.2%. Transparent to operator — low Delight (2).

entries.statement: Minimize the likelihood of systematic measurement overestimation caused by air entrainment exceeding 2% by volume in recirculation piping during oxygenation injection or post-maintenance air purging
Minimize the duration of total measurement blackout during pipe partially-filled states that occur during RAS circuit re-filling, pump restart sequences, or gravity-drain maintenance events
Minimize the measurement accuracy degradation caused by turbulent flow profiles from elbows, valves, and tee fittings within 5-10 pipe diameters upstream of the sensor in compact RAS pipe layouts
Minimize the residual flow measurement error during rapid water temperature changes exceeding 5 degrees C per hour that outpace the speed-of-sound compensation algorithm response rate
Minimize the probability of undetected flow reduction events where actual flow rate drops below the sensor minimum detection threshold in small-diameter nursery and fry-rearing feed lines

step_11_vendor_feature_market_fit.vendor_product: US Flow Sensor (DN20 serial, DN12/DN14/DN25 development)

entries.feature_description: Serve multiple pipe sizes via interchangeable flow bodies sharing a common electronics platform
entries.rationale: Vendor: DN12-DN25 covers most individual tank feed lines (mid-scale, hatchery). Incumbent: DN10 to DN3000+ covers all RAS piping. Vendor cannot serve main recirculation headers DN50-DN150. Within DN14-DN25, adequate but limited to 4 variants vs continuous range.

entries.feature_description: Support multiple digital and analog output protocols for host controller integration
entries.rationale: Vendor: UART, LIN, frequency output — LIN is automotive-focused, missing 4-20 mA + Modbus standard in aquaculture SCADA. Frequency output provides workable PLC counter-input path. Incumbent: 4-20 mA + HART + Modbus + Profibus + Profinet. Protocol mismatch is a real integration barrier.

entries.feature_description: Accept threaded or plug-in pipe connections for flexible installation
entries.rationale: Vendor: threaded and plug-in covers standard PVC/PE piping (BSP/NPT) in most RAS. Plug-in quick-connect advantageous for rapid swap-out. Incumbent: flanged, wafer, threaded, sanitary (tri-clamp), custom. Larger RAS use flanged on main headers; food-grade areas may need sanitary fittings.

entries.feature_description: Operate at ultra-low current consumption for battery-powered remote and backup monitoring
entries.rationale: Vendor: <5 uA standby enables multi-year battery operation. Enables new use cases: remote intake monitoring, emergency backup during power failure (DO crash risk), off-grid pond installations. Incumbent: electromagnetic requires 10-20 W continuous; no battery mode exists. Unique capability.

entries.feature_description: Ship fully calibrated and tested to customer-specific medium and flow profile
entries.rationale: Vendor: individual calibration to customer-specified medium (freshwater/brackish); eliminates field calibration. Valuable for aquaculture operators lacking calibration equipment. Incumbent: factory-calibrated but often requires field zero-point verification. Practical difference is modest.

entries.feature_description: Provide certified potable water contact approval for wetted sensor materials
entries.rationale: KTW/WRAS designed for human drinking water, not fish-rearing water. Indirect value: confirms non-toxic wetted materials. No aquaculture regulation requires KTW/WRAS. Both vendor and incumbents have various food/water contact certifications with partial overlap. Indifferent in this market.

Minimize the duration of total measurement blackout during pipe partially-filled
Minimize the measurement accuracy degradation caused by turbulent flow profiles
Minimize the residual flow measurement error during rapid water temperature changes
